lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Ahoy, young [lad/lass]. Might ye be able to help me, perhaps? I be in a spot of bother.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: I be the Corsair Captain! Captain of the Corsairs of Corsair Cove! My gallant band of corsairs are famed in song and story among all seafarers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've never heard of you.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr... well, ye can boast of yer ignorance if ye likes, but it don't make ye sound smarter.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Well, as I were saying, young [lad/lass], I be in a spot of bother, and I thought ye might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's very interesting; do tell me more.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Well, as I were saying, young [lad/lass], I be in a spot of bother, and I thought ye might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of help do you need?

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: It be about my crew. They be a lazy bunch at times, but they be stout in the heart and strong in the arm, and they don't deserve what's happened to them...

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: It be a curse! A curse has been laid upon my crew! That's the only thing it can be.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: So I need a young [lad/lass] such as yerself to find a way to lift the curse. Will ye help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, I'll try to help with your curse.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr! Thanks, young [lad/lass]. And a worthy [lad/lass] ye must surely be, brave in the heart and stiff in the spine.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Now, I've a ship moored west of Rimmington. Come and meet me on board, and we'll set off to Corsair Cove.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: West of Rimmington, you say? Okay, I'll meet you on board your ship there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not available.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr. Some other time, perhaps...

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, ye've found my ship - good [lad/lass]. I used to command a much larger vessel, sailing the seas with my crew of Corsairs, but now we be settled in Corsair Cove.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: My Corsairs be a good crew, but this curse has laid them low, all except me. Sickly, they be. Too weak to stand. Ye'll see fer yerself soon enough.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: There be four of 'em afflicted by the curse - Gnocci, Ithoi, Arsen and young Colin. All taken to their beds, a- mewlin' and a-pukin' like so many babbies.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: I've another crewman, Francois, but he be away on a mission. I hope he be spared!

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Now be ye ready to voyage to Corsair Cove to help 'em? I can sail ye back here any time ye likes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'm ready go to Corsair Cove.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not just now, thanks.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: That's fair enough, young [lad/lass], but perhaps ye'll return to help me soon...

sections.title: Talking to Arsen before talking to Ithoi

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Urgh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: I think so. That navigator, Ithoi, always warned us that this kind of thing could happen, but I didn't think it could possibly be real. But now this has happened, and I think it's my fault.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: You should maybe see what Ithoi thinks. He's been cursed too, so I guess he'll be in his hut, off to the south-west, near the shore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urgh to you too, with candy sprinkles on.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: A sympathetic soul you are. I'm in real pain here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I hear you've been cursed.

sections.title: Talking to Colin before talking to Ithoi

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I'm sorry! Tell her I'm sorry! Then maybe she'll make the pain stop...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Yes, that's what it must be. Ithoi, the navigator, always told us to beware of curses. You should talk to him - he really knows about these things.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Ithoi lives in the hut to the south-west, nearest to the sea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps you can tell me about it another time.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I'll be here. I'm not going anywhere while I feel so sick.

sections.title: Talking to Gnocci before talking to Ithoi

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: My stomach!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Yes, I think She did this to us. I should have left Her alone. Ithoi told us to beware of this kind of thing.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Ithoi's been cursed too. You can probably find him in his hut, south-west from here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My lower intestine!

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: ... look, I'm in a lot of pain here. We're not playing Name Your Favourite Body Part.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr, we be cursed, we be CURSED! It be a curse upon us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Aye? D'ye think so? Really? Am I not a picture of health an' beauty?

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: YES - WE'VE BEEN CURSED!

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: I've sailed the seas all my life. I sailed the seas as a man, like I sailed 'em as a boy.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: I even sailed the seas during that tricky phase in between, when ye'r not quite a man yet, but ye'r not really a boy anymore. But I don't want to talk about that bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um...

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: The point I'm makin' is that I know the seas like I know the palm of my hand. I've been to strange lands an' seen things that'll make yer juices turn in yer veins.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: I've seen dancing fish-women, monkey-kings, huge men in turbans from the far West selling treasure maps - you name it, an' I've seen it.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: There be more things in the world than ye dreams of in yer fancy book-learnin'! An' most of 'em be hostile. Could be any number of things brought this curse upon us. Demons, evil spirits, anything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, it doesn't sound like you know what's behind the curse. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll leave you to get on with it.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: ... arrr.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Tock after Ithoi

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr!

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Have ye any idea about how to lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've spoken to some of your crew.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr! Make sure ye speaks to 'em all. They'll be in their huts, over to the west. Four of them, there are - Gnocci, Ithoi, Arsen and young Colin.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Have ye any idea about how to lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Have you got that relic back from Captain Tock? Maybe if you return it to the ogres, they'll lift the curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, I've not done that.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Oh dear.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Please take it back to the ogres for me, and tell them I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Urgh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: I think so. That navigator, Ithoi, always warned us that this kind of thing could happen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met him. He definitely thinks it's a curse, but wasn't able to say exactly what caused it.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: I'm afraid it may be all my fault.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Captain Tock had me exploring some caves, west of the cove. I'm a thief, see, like my brother Francois, and the Captain thought I could sneak past the monsters without being noticed.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Well, I climbed down the vines, and found a big cave filled with terrifying ogres!

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: I'd hoped I could use my amazing Thieving skills to avoid them seeing me, but I was out of luck. By the time I'd finished clambering down the ladder, they'd spotted me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you were trying to explore that ogre cave, and the ogres saw you?

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Yes. I quickly grabbed some loot, so I wouldn't have to face the Captain empty-handed, and fled up the ladder before the ogres could kill me!

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: But a couple of days later we all fell ill. I think what I stole must have been some kind of sacred relic, and the shamans in the cave have cursed us for stealing it.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Maybe you could get the relic back from the Captain, and return it to the ogres? And tell them I'm very sorry. Then perhaps they'll lift the curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urgh to you too, with candy sprinkles on.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: A sympathetic soul you are. I'm in real pain here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Have ye any idea about how to lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arsen says he gave you a sacred ogre relic.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, that he did. I'd sent him to explore the caves west of the cove, an' he brings me back a bone lump, saying he stole it from the ogre chief.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He thinks the ogres have cursed you all because he stole one of their sacred relics, and he wants me to take it back to them now.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, I've no use fer the silly thing. If it'll get the curse lifted, ye can take it right to that ogre chief an' give it back.

sections.title: Talking to Chief Tess before talking to Captain Tock

lines.speaker: Chief Tess
lines.text: What you want, skinny creature?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A man called Arsen told me he'd visited your cave.

lines.speaker: Chief Tess
lines.text: Yah, skinny man climb down vine the other day. Little man, big idiot! Hahahaha...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He says he's very sorry...

lines.speaker: Chief Tess
lines.text: He very silly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I should come back another time.

sections.title: Talking to Chief Tess after talking to Captain Tock

lines.speaker: Chief Tess
lines.text: What you want, skinny creature?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come to return what Arsen stole.

lines.speaker: Chief Tess
lines.text: Arsen says he's very sorry for stealing from you. Here it is...

lines.speaker: Chief Tess
lines.text: Yah, dat little man is big idiot. Climbed down vine, grabbed tooth-pick off Tess and ran away like scared kebbit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're saying this is a tooth-pick? Not a sacred relic of the ogres? Did your shamans curse the Corsairs for stealing it?

lines.speaker: Chief Tess
lines.text: Dis is tooth-pick. Tess got plenty more. If skinny idiot man want Tess' old tooth-pick, maybe he sick in his head. But Tess not having shamans curse anyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Well, I guess Arsen will be pleased to hear he didn't bring the curse upon the Corsairs.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Have you returned the sacred relic to the ogres? Will they forgive me and lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Their chief, Tess, says that wasn't a sacred relic at all. It was her tooth-pick.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Are you telling me the ogres cursed us all because I stole their chief's tooth-pick?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She didn't care - she's got lots of tooth-picks, and she just thought you were some kind of weirdo when you stole one. They never cursed you at all.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Oh. That's a relief, I guess. Um... thank you for your help. But I do wonder where the curse really came from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not done investigating yet.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Have you found the mermaid yet? Will she forgive me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you find this mermaid?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I was using Ithoi's telescope to look out at the sea, imagining the strange lands and fantastic creatures Ithoi's always telling us he's seen, and I saw her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, I've not seen any mermaids round here.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Oh dear, oh dear. Maybe we'll be cursed forever!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I'm sorry! Tell her I'm sorry! Then maybe she'll make the pain stop...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Yes, that's what it must be. Ithoi, the navigator, always told us to beware of curses.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met him. He definitely thinks it's a curse, but wasn't able to say exactly what caused it.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: This is all my fault. But I didn't mean to upset her. I just thought we could be friends. Now I must have upset her, and she's cursed us all!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Ithoi always told us that bad things happen to sailors who get involved with mermaids, but I didn't believe him. I should have listened! But I'll tell you what happened...

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I was using Ithoi's telescope...

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Such a beautiful night!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: What lands lie beyond the dim horizon, I wonder...

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Eh?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: What was that? I'd better have another look.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Is that what I think it is?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: It's so dark out there, but I think... ... yes, I'm quite sure...

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: ... yes, it's a mermaid! She's beautiful!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Can you hear me?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: You're the most lovely person I've ever seen!

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I can see you moving your arms, but it's very dark. Are you waving to me?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: *kiss*

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Oh, no, I think she's gone away. Did I put her off?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Maybe she doesn't like strangers shouting about her appearance and blowing kisses at her. That might have been rude of me.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I hope she's not angry. Ithoi says mermaids can be dangerous.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Perhaps you can find the mermaid and tell her I'm very sorry I was rude to her. Then maybe she'll lift the curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps you can tell me about it another time.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I'll be here. I'm not going anywhere while I feel so sick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's see if I can find that mermaid he saw...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't look anything like a mermaid. That's an ogre!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't look anything like a mermaid. That's Bugs, the son of that chompy hunter, Rantz!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've seen enough of that ogre already. Colin might be relieved to hear it wasn't a mermaid.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Have you found the mermaid yet? Will she forgive me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've looked through that telescope myself. That wasn't a mermaid. That was a wet ogre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's definitely not a mermaid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met that ogre before. He lives with his dad in the Feldip Hills. He's definitely not a mermaid.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Oh. Oh, I see. Okay... I guess that means I didn't upset a mermaid after all. So the curse isn't my fault! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: I still feel awful. But I'm glad you've proven it wasn't my fault that we got cursed. I hope you can work out what brought it on us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be on the case.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Have you tried to destroy Her yet? Her eyes, those terrible eyes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you say this happened?

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: On the beach, to the west of the cove. Near a big tree. I buried Her in the sand, but I can still feel her eyes staring into my soul. Please find Her, and kill the demon inside!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I've not.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Oh dear, I feel terrible. Please help us soon.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: My stomach!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Yes, I think She did this to us. I should have left Her alone. Ithoi told us to beware of this kind of thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met him. He definitely thinks it's a curse, but wasn't able to say exactly what caused it.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: It's all my fault, I know it is. I've awoken Her, and now She's done this to us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: I'll explain... See, I'm the cook, and I have to find the food as well as cooking it...

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: I was fishing...

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Let's see what I can find for the boys tonight.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: C'mon little lobsters, come to Gnocci!

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Obby-lobby obby-lobby oi oi oi!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'obby-lobby' - what?

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: It's a traditional lobster-fishing chant handed down by my ancestors. Don't interrupt.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: C'mon little lobsters. We don't want to keep the Captain waiting! He'll get angry with me again.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Ooh, what's this? This isn't a lobster.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Looks more like a child's toy. But - no, it can't be...

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Is it moving?

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: She's moving! SHE'S POSSESSED! HELP!

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: I buried Her in the sand, by that tree, to the west. But I still feel Her eyes staring into my soul. Please find Her, and kill the demon inside!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My lower intestine!

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: ... look, I'm in a lot of pain here. We're not playing Name Your Favourite Body Part.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I hear you've been cursed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, he said he buried the possessed doll in the sand...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not possessed. That sounds more like clockwork.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not possessed. That sounds more like clockwork. In fact I've got the Crafting level to make those.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, it sounds scary.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Have you tried to destroy Her yet? Her eyes, those terrible eyes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, sort of. That doll wasn't possessed at all. It was a clockwork toy. They're supposed to move.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Clockwork? Oh, that would explain how it moved. Ahem. Well, I suppose the curse didn't come from Her.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: I hope you can find the real cause soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still trying.

sections.title: Talking to Ithoi during the investigation of the "curses"

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Ye be back? Tell me, do those scurvy Corsairs take the curse seriously?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been hearing about what might have caused it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Colin thought he upset a mermaid, and she might have cursed you all in revenge.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr! That'll teach him to meddle with such folk. Colin should stick to what he's good at.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gnocci fished a doll out of the water, and he thought it might have been possessed by an evil spirit.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr chuckie chuckie chuckie! That'll teach him to play with dolls at his age. Dolls can be frightening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arsen stole what he thought was a sacred relic from the ogre cave, and he said their shamans might have cursed you in revenge.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr! Ogre women be powerful, an' Arsen be a fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll carry on trying to solve the curse.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr! Off ye goes...

sections.title: Talking to Captain Tock during the investigation of the "curses"

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Have ye any idea about how to lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They all have different theories about the curse.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, that be likely enough. Ithoi tells me there be many things in this world that can bring a curse upon an unwary seafarer.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Ye can maybe solve the problem fer each victim, an' see if it lifts the curse.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Have ye any idea about how to lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said it began after they'd all eaten.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr. I'd gone away for a parlay with Ithoi; we came back ter find the crew all a-pukin' up their dinner. Then Ithoi felt ill too, though he'd not eaten with 'em, so it can't be the food.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. You're saying it began after they'd all eaten, but Ithoi felt ill even though he'd not had any of the food.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, that be right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That meal must have been interesting...

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Have ye any idea about how to lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've ruled out all the Corsairs' theories...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cabin-boy Colin thought he'd upset a mermaid, but there wasn't a mermaid, just a wet ogre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arsen the Thief thought he'd upset the ogre shamans by stealing a sacred ogre relic, but it was just a tooth-pick, and the ogres didn't care.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ithoi the Navigator said there were lots of things that could bring a curse on a crew, and this could be any of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Gnocci the Cook thought he'd awoken a demon in a possessed doll, but it wasn't possessed at all. It was clockwork.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, any sufficiently advanced technology be indistinguishable from demonic possession, that it be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I do now?

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, I don't rightly know. It began when I'd gone away for a parlay with Ithoi; we came back ter find the crew all a-pukin' up their dinner. Then Ithoi felt ill too, though he'd not eaten with 'em, so it can't be the food.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. You're saying it began after they'd all eaten, but Ithoi felt ill even though he'd not had any of the food.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, that be right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That meal must have been interesting...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I give up and go home?

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: If ye wants a ride back to Rimmington, I'll take ye there any time, but I do hope ye can help my poor crew.

sections.title: Talking to Ithoi before talking to everyone else

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Ye be back? Have ye been investigatin' the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear it happened straight after dinner.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr! That be wrong! I were away for a parlay with Captain Tock, an' I didn't eat that dinner at all. The Captain can tell ye so himself.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: So it must be a curse. A CURSE!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Have you solved the curse yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you say Ithoi prepared that meal?

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Yes, he volunteered to do all the fishing and cooking for me, even though he knew he'd miss dinner for his parlay with Captain Tock. Ithoi's a good soul.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Have you solved the curse yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear it happened straight after dinner.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Hey, don't go blaming the cook just because the curse began after dinner. I didn't even cook that meal. I'd been too scared of the possessed doll...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean the clockwork doll?

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: It's an easy mistake to make!

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Anyway I didn't dare try to fish after that, and Ithoi volunteered to do all the fishing and cooking instead, before he went off for his parlay with Captain Tock.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Anyway, Ithoi got sick without even eating it, so it can't be the food.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear the Captain's thinking of firing Ithoi.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: I don't see why that's got anything to do with the curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Have you found out how we all got cursed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear the Captain's thinking of firing Ithoi.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Well, I suppose we don't need a navigator these days, and he's not very good at anything else. But I don't see why that's got anything to do with the curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Have you found out how we all got cursed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear it happened straight after dinner.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: That's right. Gnocci had cooked us some swordfish. It didn't taste as good as his usual cooking, but I didn't complain. The Captain doesn't like complainers.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Then we all started feeling sick and clammy. That was when the Captain and Ithoi came back from their parlay, and Ithoi looked sick too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Do you know how we all got cursed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear Ithoi cooked the meal you ate that night.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: I suppose Ithoi's trying to be extra helpful at the moment. Don't tell anyone, but Ithoi might not be in our crew much longer.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: My brother, Francois, says the Captain will make him second-in-command if he completes his mission. That's Ithoi's rank, but we don't need a navigator now we've settled here, and he can't do much else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the mission Francois is doing?

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: That's a secret.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Tock after talking to everyone else

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Have ye any idea about how to lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you're thinking of firing Ithoi.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr, maybe I am, but I'm sure that be nothing to do wi' the curse. Ithoi's afflicted by it too.

sections.title: Talking to Ithoi after talking to everyone else
Talking to Gnocchi after Ithoi admits he poisoned the crew

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Have you solved the curse yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ithoi admits poisoning you all and faking the curse.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: If Ithoi's been afflicted by the curse as badly as I have, he might be half out of his mind with pain. I'm sure he doesn't know what he's saying.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it.

sections.title: Talking to Colin after Ithoi admits he poisoned the crew

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Have you solved the curse yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ithoi admits poisoning you all and faking the curse.

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: That can't be right - he looks as sick as the rest of us. Maybe he's delerious and doesn't know what he's saying.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it.

sections.title: Talking to Arsen after Ithoi admits he poisoned the crew

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Do you know how we all got cursed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ithoi admits poisoning you all and faking the curse.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: He's had a lot to worry about, with losing his place on the crew, and now he's sick from the curse too. He must have gone mad and started babbling.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Tock after Ithoi admits he poisoned the crew

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Have ye any idea about how to lift the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ithoi admits he poisoned the crew! He's faked this whole curse!

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: That can't be! Ithoi's sick, an' he may be babbling madness from his fevered brain. Ye'll have to prove he's not really sick.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: What d'ye want now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know you've faked the curse.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: The Corsairs all think I'm sick too, even though I never ate the food. An' ye'll never make me prove that I'm healthy - I ain't gettin' up! So they'll keep believing in the curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I bet I can prove you're well enough to get up.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Ye'll never make me get up! I ain't afraid of cold, nor of wet, nor of anythin' ye can do in here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... nothing, really.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Ye be back? Have ye been investigatin' the curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you cooked the meal they ate before getting sick.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr? And what if I did? Can't an old sailor help his crew without interfering young land-lubbers making accusations?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe because the Captain's thinking of firing you - Arsen heard it from his brother. The Corsairs don't need a navigator anymore.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr! Too clever ye be... I heard the Captain might kick me off the crew, so I poisoned the dinner, an' acted sick too. Now they think they're cursed, so they'll want me around, wi' my knowledge o' such things!

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: But they all think I'm sick, so they'll never believe I'm behind it. Hahaha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see what they have to say about that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe you're just a nasty person.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Bah! Ye can't even think of a reason why I'd want to poison the crew, so don't bother me with yer accusations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know your reason.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Bah! Ye can't even think of a reason why I'd want to poison the crew, so don't bother me with yer accusations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into it.

sections.title: Attempting to light the the driftwood before discovering Ithoi's lie

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I should try to get more information out of him before trying to make his hut catch fire.

sections.title: Attempting to light the the driftwood after discovering Ithoi's lie

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: What d'ye want now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I saw you running around! You're not sick!

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: So long as that old fool, Tock, believes it's a curse, I've nothing to fear from ye flapping yer mouth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see what the Captain has to say about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... nothing, really.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr... uh-oh!

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess that proves he's well enough to get up! And apparently he knows some magic too.

sections.title: Talking to Gnocci after proving Ithoi poisoned the crew

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Can you tell me what's going on now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ithoi's faked the curse. I've seen him running around, healthy.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Make sure he gets what's coming to him. No-one poisons my crew!

sections.title: Talking to Colin after proving Ithoi poisoned the crew

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: So what's going on now?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: It sounds like there isn't a curse after all! Maybe I'll recover soon from whatever he did to me.

sections.title: Talking to Arsen after proving Ithoi poisoned the crew

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: What's happening now?

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: Give him what he deserves!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Tock after proving Ithoi poisoned the crew

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: So what's happening now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've seen Ithoi running around. He's not sick at all.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Arrr! So he was never sick at all! An' here's me been feeling sorry for him. A trickster he be indeed! Kill him fer me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you kill him yourself?

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: A captain shouldn't leave his ship in a time of crisis. Rule of the sea, that is. Even if the crisis ain't exactly on the ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't believe in capital punishment.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back.

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Ye'll pay!

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Curse you!

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Curse 'em all!

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Arrr, land-lubber!

lines.speaker: Ithoi the Navigator
lines.text: Awww!

sections.title: Talking to Gnocci after killing Ithoi

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: Can you tell me what's going on now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ithoi faked the curse, and I've killed him.

lines.speaker: Gnocci the Cook
lines.text: You think you know a guy, and then he does a thing like this! Oh well, I hope I recover soon from whatever he did to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not much.

sections.title: Talking to Colin after killing Ithoi

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: So what's going on now?

lines.speaker: Cabin Boy Colin
lines.text: Who'd have thought Ithoi would do such a thing? I guess if there isn't really a curse, I'll get better soon. In fact I feel better already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not much.

sections.title: Talking to Arsen after killing Ithoi

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: What's happening now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ithoi faked the curse, and I've killed him.

lines.speaker: Arsen the Thief
lines.text: I really didn't think he was like that. Still, if there isn't really a curse, I suppose we'll just get better from whatever he did to us. I don't feel so bad now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not much.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Tock after killing Ithoi

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: So what's happening now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've killed Ithoi for poisoning your crew.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Thank ye fer dealing with that serpent. I suppose the rest of my crew will feel better now ye've proven there be no curse on them.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Most of them deleterious effects probably be psychosomatic anyway, arrr!

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Anyways, ye've been a mighty help, young [lad/lass]. Ye be brave in the heart, strong in the arm and full in the bowels, an' I be grateful to ye.

lines.speaker: Captain Tock
lines.text: Tell ye what, I'll have a word with Yusuf, in the bank. Ye've earned the right to use his services. An' thanks again, very much.
